## CADO-NFS 3.1.0-modern

The first release on the fork's **own minor line**. It keeps upstream
[CADO-NFS](https://gitlab.inria.fr/cado-nfs/cado-nfs) **3.0.0**'s NFS mathematics
unchanged and builds on a current toolchain (CMake 4.x · GCC 16 · C++20 · CUDA
13 · Python 3.14), adding **GPU linear algebra**, a **GPU pre-NFS ECM
front-end**, **AVX-512 (VPCLMULQDQ + IFMA) kernels**, and a batch of
**orchestration / UX** features.

> **No NFS mathematics is changed.** Every track is an implementation, build, or
> orchestration optimization. The gate after each change is correctness —
> `make check` plus a verified `product == N` factorization, or a bit-exact
> validation against a reference (GMP / the CPU path / Intel SDE). Results are
> reported honestly, **including the parts that did not pay off**, and
> hardware-blocked items are shipped as **documented designs**, not unvalidated
> code.

### GPU linear algebra (the headline)

- **Real `mm_impl=gpu` BWC SpMV backend** (`linalg/bwc/matmul-gpu.cu`) plugged
  into CADO's matmul dispatch, with both the matrix and its transpose resident on
  the device as CSR and a coalesced warp-per-row kernel. Bit-exact
  (`bench_matcache` 4/4) and end-to-end `product == N`.
- **Full vector residency** — the steady krylov/mksol/secure loop runs entirely
  on device buffers (the 2D reduce+broadcast comm, GPU `x_dotprod`, and a device
  `addmul_tiny` all ported), eliminating the per-iteration PCIe transfers that
  were ~60% of SpMV time. `product == N` across default / `DEVCOMM` /
  `VECRESIDENT+DEVCOMM` × `-t 2/4/8`; compute-sanitizer clean.
- **Measured win grows with N.** GPU warp SpMV holds **7.9 Gnz/s at c120-scale**
  (240M nnz, bit-exact) while the CPU reference loop collapses — 18×→41× over the
  reference loop across 1M→8M rows, a steadier ~4.4× over the tuned `bucket`
  backend's saturated ~1.8 Gnz/s. (`BENCHMARKS.md`, `docs/gpu-linalg.md`.)
- **Multi-node MPI + per-rank multi-GPU**, and an **intra-node multi-GPU matrix
  partition** (`CADO_GPU_NPART`, default off). Fixed a latent
  `bench_matcache`-vs-GPU-backend static-link cycle along the way.

### GPU pre-NFS factoring front-end (validated)

- **`cado-nfs.py --gpu-prefactor`** strips small/medium factors with batched
  multi-precision GPU ECM (stage-1 + stage-2 BSGS + Suyama, multi-GPU,
  escalating-`B1` schedule) before NFS — a *separate* stage with no Amdahl
  ceiling (unlike in-sieve cofactorization). **~49× / 26× / 12×** the full CPU at
  128/256/512-bit on an RTX 3090; end-to-end `product == N`.
  (`docs/gpu-prefactor.md`.)

### AVX-512 kernels (correctness-only, CI-gated under SDE)

- **VPCLMULQDQ gf2x** auto-detection (`configure.ac`/`features.m4`, run-test-gated
  so a non-AVX-512 host safely keeps the pclmul backend); hot `mul1` bit-exact
  under Intel SDE.
- **IFMA GF(p) Montgomery modmul** kernel (`bench/ifma-modmul.c`), 8-way batched
  radix-2^52 — bit-exact vs GMP under SDE (0/32000). Both are gated by a new
  `avx512-validate.yml` CI workflow.

### Orchestration / UX

- **Parameter interpolation** + `--suggest-params`: off-preset sizes get
  interpolated parameters instead of an error (`product == N` on a real c45).
- **`/status` endpoint + `/dashboard`** on both the Flask and Rust work-unit
  servers; live phase / progress / ETA / factors.
- **clap CLIs** for both Rust binaries (`--help`/validation, semantics
  preserved), a **cluster-launch** helper (SSH / Slurm), and a **ratatui terminal
  monitor** (`cado-nfs-monitor-rs`).

### Honest negatives & documented designs

- **PGO rejected again** (siever-trained, +3.0%) and **no safe hot-scalar
  micro-opt** found (the hot loops are already SIMD/unrolled/prefetched) — both
  recorded with measurements.
- **Multi-node residency transfer-win, cofactor scale-out, GPU product-tree** are
  documented as concrete designs (with the transfer accounting that shows why the
  naive multi-node version yields no win); they need 2+ GPUs / a cluster / DLP
  set-ups to validate, so they are specified rather than shipped as unvalidated
  code.

### Build / run

```bash
bash scripts/setup-venv.sh        # one-time: Flask + requests venv
make                              # out-of-tree build via local.sh
cado-nfs.venv/bin/python3 ./cado-nfs.py <N>            # factor N
# GPU build: set ENABLE_GPU=ON in local.sh; then tasks.linalg.bwc.mm_impl=gpu,
# CADO_GPU_VECRESIDENT=1 CADO_GPU_DEVCOMM=1, and/or ./cado-nfs.py <N> --gpu-prefactor
```

Toolchain floor (from 3.0.0): C99 + C++20; GCC ≥ 10 / Clang ≥ 12; CMake ≥ 3.18;
GMP ≥ 5 built `--enable-shared`. Optional: CUDA (GPU tracks), MPI, hwloc, curl.

LGPL-2.1, as upstream. See `CHANGELOG.md` for the full per-track detail and
`docs/` for the deep-dives (`gpu-linalg.md`, `gpu-prefactor.md`,
`gpu-cofactorization.md`, `rust-orchestration.md`).
